extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won a bunch sprint by half a wheel over EWAN Caleb after Jumbo-Visma delivered him perfectly positioned through final kilometer
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma's lead-out displaced Sagan's Bora train entirely, forcing Sagan to freelance through closing 500m.
Viviani locked onto Deceuninck lead-out through Morkov-Richeze but positioning at 1km proved more critical than train horsepower.
EWAN Caleb demonstrated superior acceleration in final 150m despite worse positioning, nearly erasing half-length deficit to Groenewegen.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: displaced from his own Bora train in final kilometer, forced to navigate traffic alone without teammate Daniel Oss
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: bounced back from Brussels crash with perfect positioning discipline, stayed patient behind Jumbo-Visma leadout
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: raw acceleration in final 150m nearly compensated for poor positioning, half-wheel margin suggests fastest pure speed
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: negative: significant crash at 7km into stage hitting road sign caused facial bleeding but continued racing
